source_log.detail: User/main thread supplied K group final-score focus: Colombia 0-0 Portugal and DR Congo 3-1 Uzbekistan.

handicap_result.real_condition: Colombia draw, so Colombia +1 wins after adjustment

k_group_leg_settlement.settlement_reason: The real match was 0-0, which satisfies Colombia real win/draw for +1 handicap win.
k_group_leg_settlement.notes: This leg correctly captured draw-management and Portugal non-win risk.

k_group_leg_settlement.settlement_reason: The selected leg required DR Congo draw/loss in the real match; actual DR Congo won by 2.
k_group_leg_settlement.notes: The model treated non-cover as value, but 3-1 showed the must-win/two-plus margin tail was under-protected.

k_group_leg_settlement.settlement_reason: The leg required Colombia to win; actual match was 0-0.
k_group_leg_settlement.notes: The draw was a live model path and a key third-round script.

k_group_leg_settlement.settlement_reason: The leg required DR Congo by exactly 1; actual margin was 2.
k_group_leg_settlement.notes: The one-goal corridor was too narrow for a must-win match with Uzbekistan chase risk.

k_group_leg_settlement.settlement_reason: The leg required Uzbekistan to win; actual DR Congo won 3-1.
k_group_leg_settlement.notes: The underdog-tail branch was overwhelmed by DR Congo direction and margin.

k_group_leg_settlement.stake_scope: R1 user stake 16 RMB; K leg only
k_group_leg_settlement.settlement_reason: 0-0 is Colombia real draw, which hits Colombia +1 handicap win.
k_group_leg_settlement.notes: The K leg worked, but it should still be described as a handicap settlement hit rather than an ordinary Colombia direction hit.

k_group_leg_settlement.stake_scope: R2 user stake 16 RMB; K leg only
k_group_leg_settlement.settlement_reason: 0-0 satisfies the wider Colombia +1 protection bucket.
k_group_leg_settlement.notes: The wide handicap protection included draw and therefore handled the 0-0 correctly.

k_group_leg_settlement.stake_scope: R2 user stake 16 RMB; K leg only
k_group_leg_settlement.settlement_reason: The leg required DR Congo by exactly 1; actual margin was 2.
k_group_leg_settlement.notes: Exact one-goal margin failed because the 2+ tail arrived.

k_group_leg_settlement.stake_scope: R3 6x4 exact-margin system; K leg contribution only
k_group_leg_settlement.settlement_reason: The leg required Portugal by exactly 1; actual match was 0-0.
k_group_leg_settlement.notes: The exact Portugal one-goal corridor ignored the 0-0 draw-management path.

k_group_leg_settlement.stake_scope: R3 6x4 exact-margin system; K leg contribution only
k_group_leg_settlement.settlement_reason: The leg required DR Congo by exactly 1; actual margin was 2.
k_group_leg_settlement.notes: The system used exact-margin corridors; K group produced one draw and one two-goal win instead.

k_group_leg_settlement.stake_scope: R5 user stake 12 RMB; K leg only
k_group_leg_settlement.settlement_reason: DR Congo won by 2 goals, matching the selected 2+ margin.
k_group_leg_settlement.notes: This was the correct K-group tail bucket, but R5 still needs non-K legs for full settlement.

069_colombia_plus1_win_or_win_draw.diagnosis: 0-0 was exactly the draw-management path that +1 protection needed to include.

069_colombia_plus1_draw.diagnosis: This is draw_floor_error and narrow exact-margin risk.

070_dr_congo_minus1_win_or_draw.diagnosis: 3-1 landed in the two-plus margin bucket, not the exact one-goal bucket.

colombia_0_0_impact.diagnosis: The 0-0 result validates draw-management and low-event risk. Ordinary Colombia win or Portugal exact one-goal legs fail; Colombia +1 protection hits.

dr_congo_3_1_impact.diagnosis: The 3-1 result validates DR Congo direction and 2+ margin tail, while invalidating non-cover and exact one-goal tickets.

direction_hits: DR Congo ordinary direction: model had DR Congo as the top 1X2 outcome and actual DR Congo won.
direction_misses_or_partial: Colombia vs Portugal ordinary model leaned Portugal, but actual was draw; draw floor was material and should not have been subordinated to Portugal direction.
draw_floor_errors: 069 exact Portugal-by-one and Colombia-win branches failed on 0-0.
Colombia +1 protection hit because it preserved the draw path.
tail_errors: 070 actual 3-1 exceeded the exact one-goal corridor and the under-2.5 lean.
DR Congo 2+ margin was a modeled tail around 24.4%, but multiple tickets treated non-cover or exact one-goal as more useful.
market_or_context_errors: K group third-round motivation made DR Congo's 2+ route more live than the non-cover construction allowed.
Colombia's draw-enough context correctly suppressed Portugal win but not necessarily Colombia win.

bet_translation_errors: Some K legs described Colombia +1 protection correctly, but later ticket narratives risked treating it as a Colombia direction call. It settled via 0-0 draw.
DR Congo -1 loss was a non-cover bucket, not a broad 'DR Congo cautious game' guarantee; 3-1 showed why the win/margin split must stay explicit.
portfolio_structure_errors: T3 paired two margin-sensitive non-cover legs; K leg failed on DR Congo 2+.
T5 combined ordinary Colombia win with exact DR Congo one-goal margin; both were too narrow for actual K outcomes.
R3 exact-margin system missed both K exact-margin legs.
hedge_independence: Colombia +1 protection was a valid K hedge, but it does not independently protect tickets if paired with fragile non-K or exact-margin legs.
DR Congo -1 win tail hit in R5, but other legs can still make the full ticket fail.
narrow_path_risk: 069 0-0 proves draw floor must be retained in top-race matches.
070 3-1 proves must-win favorites can jump from one-goal corridor to two-plus with chase-state help.

rule_updates.update: When a team can achieve its group objective with a draw, ordinary opponent-win and exact opponent-by-one legs must carry draw_floor_error risk by default.
A +1 handicap win that hits on 0-0 must be recorded as handicap protection, not as a match-direction hit.
For must-win teams facing an opponent that must chase, keep the 2+ margin bucket visible even when market value appears to favor non-cover.
Exact one-goal margin legs such as Colombia +1 draw or DR Congo -1 draw should remain high-variance and must not be stacked heavily across several matches.
K legs can hit while the full ticket misses elsewhere; postmatch review must separate leg settlement from ticket settlement.
Post-result reviews update risk rules only and must not propose new purchase paths from observed outcomes.

final_verdict.summary: K-group lesson is a split one: Colombia +1 protection worked because it preserved 0-0, while DR Congo non-cover and exact one-goal structures failed because 3-1 landed in the two-plus margin tail.
